commit.message:
ci: fix release pipeline — merge version-bump into release workflow (#1)

* ci: merge version-bump into release workflow

Fix critical bug: version-bump.yml created tags using GITHUB_TOKEN,
but events from GITHUB_TOKEN don't trigger other workflows, so
release.yml would never fire.

Now a single release workflow handles everything on push to master:
1. Determine version bump type (conventional commits)
2. Compute next semver version
3. Build, sign, and publish to Maven Central + GitHub Packages
4. Commit version bump + create tag
5. Create GitHub Release

CI workflow now only runs on PRs (validation before merge).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector with direct ray-plane test for planar faces (#233)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_Intersector with direct ray-plane test for planar faces

For faces that are planar and have all-straight-line edges, precompute the
UV polygon at construction time (ComputeBounds) and use a direct ray-plane
intersection + 2D point-in-polygon test instead of IntCurvesFace_Intersector::Perform.

IntCurvesFace_Intersector::Perform dominated the B4c callgrind profile at
~1052M instructions (50% of total): the actual line-plane intersection
(IntAna_IntConicQuad) was only ~18M self-instructions; the rest was
overhead from TopExp_Explorer wire traversal, BRepTopAdaptor_FClass2d /
CSLib_Class2d 2D containment, NCollection_Sequence heap alloc/free, and
GeomAdaptor reconstruction on every call.

The fast path:
- RayPlaneFaceHit(): explicit dot-product ray-plane intersection, then
  ElSLib::PlaneParameters to get UV, then ray-casting PointInPolygon2d.
- PointOnPolygonBoundary2d(): detects UV hits within tolerance of any
  polygon edge, triggering the same degenerateRay fallback as TopAbs_ON
  does in the OCCT intersector path (preserves kSurface classification).
- FaceBounds gains uvPolygon (precomputed outer-wire vertices in UV space)
  and outwardNormal (constant outward normal for planar faces, eliminating
  TryGetOutwardNormal / BRepLProp_SLProps calls in DistanceToOut).

Applied in Inside(p), DistanceToIn(p,v), and DistanceToOut(p,v,...).
Non-planar faces and planar faces with curved edges fall back to the
existing IntCurvesFace_Intersector path unchanged.

commit.message:
fix: restore Dictionary return from FetchAndCacheAllAsync, increase timeout to 15s

Root cause: FetchAndCacheAllAsync was refactored to return void Task instead
of Dictionary<string,string>. PrepareScreensaverData read files from disk
as fallback, but the 5s timeout expired before files were written on first
run, resulting in no data injected into modules.

Fix:
- Restore Dictionary<string,string> return value from FetchAndCacheAllAsync
- Use return value directly in PrepareScreensaverData (in-memory, no race)
- Increase timeout from 5s to 15s (7 GraphQL years take time)
- Fall back to reading cached files only when timeout expires
- Guard against serializing undefined JsonElement values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix #242: enforce shulker entity limit on Paper via ShulkerDuplicateEvent

On Paper servers, CreatureSpawnEvent fires for the new shulker *after* the
original shulker has already teleported away. If the original shulker
teleports outside the island bounding box, getNearbyEntities counts N-1
shulkers instead of N, so the limit check incorrectly allows duplication
past the configured limit.

Paper's ShulkerDuplicateEvent fires *before* the original shulker teleports
and before the duplicate is created, so the island entity count is accurate.
Register PaperShulkerLimitListener when Paper is detected at runtime;
fall back to the existing CreatureSpawnEvent handling on Spigot.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CI compilation errors

- Change Paper API dependency from 1.21.8 to 1.20.4, which is the last
  version compiled for Java 17. Paper 1.20.6+ targets Java 21 (class
  version 65), which the compiler rejects under --release 17.
- Stage the remaining add_tags source files (IslandBlockCount,
  BlockLimitsListener, RecountCalculator, etc.) that Limits.java depends
  on; they were missing from the previous commit, causing NamespacedKey /
  Material type mismatches in CI.
- Fix @AfterEach (JUnit 5) -> @After (JUnit 4) in the three test files
  that have JUnit 4 imports; the annotation was unresolved in CI.

Merge pull request #12 from dchu3/feat/configure-paste-ux

fix: handle clear sentinel and empty RHS in key-value paste

- Check 'clear' sentinel on raw input before prefix-stripping so
  pasting KEY=clear saves the literal value instead of deleting
- Treat KEY= or KEY: with empty RHS as an explicit clear instead
  of silently keeping the old value

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(Frame): auto-hide back button on mobile, wire system back gesture

On platforms with a native back gesture (Android, iOS), the visual back
button is hidden via OnFormFactor and navigation is handled by
subscribing to TopLevel.BackRequestedEvent. This follows the same
pattern as MasterDetailsNavigator.RequiresBackButton().

The system back handler executes BackCommand when available and marks the
event as handled; if no BackCommand is set, the event bubbles up to
allow the OS default behavior (e.g., closing the app).

Desktop platforms retain the visual back button as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
